2603.23268.title: SafeSeek: Universal Attribution of Safety Circuits in Language Models
2603.23268.paper_summary: Mechanistic interpretability reveals that safety-critical behaviors (e.g., alignment, jailbreak, backdoor) in Large Language Models (LLMs) are grounded in specialized functional components. However, existing safety attribution methods struggle with generalization and reliability due to their reliance on heuristic, domain-specific metrics and search algorithms. To address this, we propose \ourmethod, a unified safety interpretability framework that identifies functionally complete safety circuits in LLMs via optimization. Unlike methods focusing on isolated heads or neurons, \ourmethod introduces differentiable binary masks to extract multi-granular circuits through gradient descent on safety datasets, while integrates Safety Circuit Tuning to utilize these sparse circuits for efficient safety fine-tuning. We validate \ourmethod in two key scenarios in LLM safety: \textbf{(1) backdoor attacks}, identifying a backdoor circuit with 0.42\% sparsity, whose ablation eradicates the Attack Success Rate (ASR) from 100\% $\to$ 0.4\% while retaining over 99\% general utility; \textbf{(2) safety alignment}, localizing an alignment circuit with 3.03\% heads and 0.79\% neurons, whose removal spikes ASR from 0.8\% $\to$ 96.9\%, whereas excluding this circuit during helpfulness fine-tuning maintains 96.5\% safety retention.
2603.23268.paper_summary_zh: 機械解釋性揭示了大型語言模型（LLMs）中安全關鍵行為（例如，對齊、越獄、後門）是基於專門的功能組件。然而，現有的安全歸因方法因依賴啟發式、特定領域的指標和搜索算法而在泛化和可靠性方面面臨挑戰。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了\ourmethod，一個統一的安全解釋框架，通過優化在LLMs中識別功能完整的安全電路。與專注於孤立頭部或神經元的方法不同，\ourmethod引入可微分的二元掩碼，通過在安全數據集上的梯度下降提取多粒度電路，同時整合安全電路調整以利用這些稀疏電路進行高效的安全微調。我們在LLM安全的兩個關鍵場景中驗證了\ourmethod：\textbf{(1) 後門攻擊}，識別出一個稀疏度為0.42\%的後門電路，其消融使攻擊成功率（ASR）從100\%降至0.4\%，同時保留超過99\%的通用效用；\textbf{(2) 安全對齊}，定位到一個稀疏度為3.03\%的對齊電路和0.79\%的神經元，其移除使ASR從0.8\%激增至96.9\%，而在有用性微調期間排除此電路則保持96.5\%的安全保留。
2603.23268.author: Miao Yu et.al.
2603.23268.authors: Miao Yu, Siyuan Fu, Moayad Aloqaily, Zhenhong Zhou, Safa Otoum, Xing fan, Kun Wang, Yufei Guo, Qingsong Wen
